title: Step Decrement Loop in While Loops

content.problemStatement:
A **step decrement loop** with a while loop repeatedly executes a block of code, each time decreasing a counter variable by a fixed amount until a specific stopping condition is met. This technique is ideal for tasks like countdowns, reversing sequences, and managing repetitive tasks in a controlled, decremental manner.

**Syntax:** 

```java
int i = initial_value;       // Initial value
while (i > limit) {          // Condition to continue the loop
    // Loop body: Code to execute on each iteration

    i -= step_decrement;     // Decrement step
}
```
---
**Example:**  
```java
int i = 10;                  // Start from 10
while (i >= 1) {             // Continue as long as i is greater than or equal to 1
    System.out.println(i);   // Print the current value of i
    i -= 2;                  // Decrement i by 2 each time (10, 8, 6, 4 , 2)
}
```

**Expected Output:** 

```default
10
8
6
4
2
```
